releaseNotes: v0.35.0 — Fallback labels now scan on small DYMO stock. Bottles without a manufacturer 2D barcode get a RxRescue fallback label; until now that label carried a 1D (Code 128) barcode whose minimum physical width doesn't fit the DYMO 450's 1"x2-1/8" stock, so it clipped at the edges and wouldn't scan (Alex's active blocker). v0.35.0 switches the fallback to a 2D DataMatrix — the same 2D symbology RxRescue's main label already uses and the app already reads natively — laid out as a square code on the left with the text column on the right. It fits with quiet-zone margin to spare and scans reliably from a phone camera or 2D handheld. Same label, same stock, same workflow: the printed code still resolves back to the exact bottle (its expiration, lot, and history) on re-scan. Existing printed labels keep working. Three-reviewer audit (self + Sonnet + Codex), no shippable-blocking findings; 715 tests passing. Auto-updater rolls v0.34.0 -> v0.35.0 on next launch.
